meaning: adj.焦躁的,附有弦马的,腐蚀的
examples: Once they made him wear a brace but he fretted so he was downright ill.
His first betrothed heard of this, and fretted so much about his faithfulness that she nearly died.
Investors also fretted over Spain and Portugal.
Shares fell 4.49%, however, as investors fretted about competition from Qualcomm, Texas Instruments and others.
Apple co-founder Steve Wozniak has even fretted about the MacBook Air's lack of an optical drive.
